mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that force monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint interaction term yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output and profit margins, generating negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of declining industrial production months, or the signal shows zero correlation with market direction.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero correlation with forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over rolling 2-year windows, or positive returns fail to exceed baseline frequency.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million units signals a broad demand contraction that reduces capital expenditure and earnings, causing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of triggered months, or the threshold signal produces a negative Sharpe ratio in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation alongside a static or rising federal funds rate indicates restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real incomes.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly GDP growth confirms a robust consumption cycle that boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive short-term equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: GDP growth signals fail to predict 5-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03) during early-cycle periods, or positive returns occur in less than 45 percent of instances.
